words.definition: A large cage, building, or enclosure for keeping birds.
words.example: The zoo's aviary housed hundreds of tropical birds.

words.definition: A place where bees are kept; a collection of beehives.
words.example: The beekeeper inspected his apiary at dawn.

words.definition: A building in which horses are kept.
words.example: The horses were led back to the stable at dusk.

words.definition: An individual compartment for one animal in a stable or shed.
words.example: Each horse had its own stall lined with fresh straw.

words.definition: A shelter for a dog, or a boarding establishment for dogs.
words.example: The puppy curled up in its warm kennel.

words.definition: A boarding place or breeding establishment for cats.
words.example: We left the cat at a cattery during the holidays.

words.definition: A pen or enclosure for pigs.
words.example: The farmer cleaned the sty every morning.

words.definition: An enclosure for pigs; a pigsty.

words.definition: A cage or pen for confining poultry, especially chickens.
words.example: She collected fresh eggs from the coop.

words.definition: A box or cage, typically with a wire mesh front, for keeping rabbits or other small animals.

words.definition: A building in which cattle are kept and milked.

words.definition: A large farm building used for storage or for housing livestock.

words.definition: A pen or enclosure for cattle, horses, or other livestock.

words.definition: A small field or enclosure where horses are kept or exercised.

words.definition: Land covered with grass suitable for the grazing of livestock.

words.definition: A piece of grassland, especially one used for hay.

words.definition: A pen or enclosure for sheep.

words.definition: A pen or enclosure in which sheep are kept.

words.definition: A small enclosure for farm animals.
words.example: The goats were kept in a wooden pen.

words.definition: A place where birds regularly settle or congregate to rest at night.
words.example: At sunset the chickens returned to their roost.

words.definition: A bar or branch on which a bird rests.

words.definition: A structure or place made or chosen by a bird for laying eggs.
words.example: Swallows built a nest under the eaves.

words.definition: A colony of breeding rooks or, more broadly, seals, penguins, or seabirds.

words.definition: A breeding colony of herons.

words.definition: A large nest of a bird of prey, especially an eagle, built high in a tree or cliff.

words.definition: A shelter with nest holes for domesticated pigeons.

words.definition: A place where pigeons are kept.

words.definition: A tank of water in which live fish and other aquatic animals are kept.

words.definition: An enclosure for keeping animals under semi-natural conditions for observation or study.

words.definition: A place where insects are kept and exhibited.

quiz.explanation: Falconry mews (noun) — A building where trained hawks or falcons are kept.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A range of stables or buildings where birds of prey were formerly kept."?
quiz.explanation: Mews (noun) — A range of stables or buildings where birds of prey were formerly kept.

quiz.question: Which word means: "The small burrow entrance made by a vole."?
quiz.explanation: Vole hole (noun) — The small burrow entrance made by a vole.

quiz.question: Which word means: "The den or burrow of a fox."?
quiz.explanation: Fox earth (noun) — The den or burrow of a fox.

quiz.question: Which word means: "The underground lair of a fox or badger."?
quiz.explanation: Earth (noun) — The underground lair of a fox or badger.

quiz.question: Which word means: "The den of an otter."?
quiz.explanation: Holt (noun) — The den of an otter.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A shelter in which an animal hibernates."?
quiz.explanation: Hibernaculum (noun) — A shelter in which an animal hibernates.

quiz.question: Which word means: "An area of land set aside for wildlife protection."?
quiz.explanation: Reserve (noun) — An area of land set aside for wildlife protection.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A public park where living animals are kept and exhibited."?
quiz.explanation: Zoological garden (noun) — A public park where living animals are kept and exhibited.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A small zoo where children can safely stroke and feed the animals."?
quiz.explanation: Petting zoo (noun) — A small zoo where children can safely stroke and feed the animals.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A facility for cultivating aquatic animals or plants for food."?
quiz.explanation: Aquaculture farm (noun) — A facility for cultivating aquatic animals or plants for food.
